title: Tornado In A Jar
description: This Mod Adds Tornados in jars, with many different abilities. This is my first mod on modrinth, so if anything buggy, im sorry.
body:
🌪️ Tornadoes in a Jar

Tornadoes in a Jar adds powerful, strange, and sometimes dangerous storms that can be held in your hand. Capture the force of nature itself and unleash it whenever you choose. From freezing snownados to blazing firenados, each tornado contains a unique effect that changes how you play the game.

This mod focuses on creative weather-based abilities, giving players new ways to explore, fight, and experiment using contained storm energy. And a bit of Error and the unknown...

🌪️ Features
🌀 Tornado Jars

Tornadoes can be contained inside special jars, allowing players to safely store and release storm energy. Each tornado type applies a different effect when used, making every jar useful in different situations.

Examples include:

Tornado — harness raw wind energy

Snownado — freezing cold storm effects

Firenado — burning winds and heat

Waterspout — water-based storm power

Twin Tornado — unstable dual vortex energy

Multi-Vortex Tornado — chaotic storm behavior

Each tornado is designed around a single clear effect, making them easy to understand and fun to combine!

⚗️ Simple Crafting

Recipes are designed to be accessible and easy to understand. Most tornado jars use common Minecraft materials, making the mod playable early in survival without long or complicated crafting chains.

🎮 Gameplay Focus

This mod is built around:

Experimentation

Creative combat options

Environmental effects

Fun storm-themed mechanics

Whether you want to collect every tornado type or just use them as tools during adventures, the mod adds new ways to interact with the world.

Minecraft 1.21.1

⚙️ Compatibility

Designed for vanilla-style gameplay and works alongside most mods that do not heavily change weather or potion systems. JEI recommended.

🚧 Work in Progress

More tornado types, effects, and storm variations may be added in future updates.

Feedback and suggestions are welcome.
